components.name: 1-Wire Bus via DS2484
components.description: The ds2484 platform provides access to 1-Wire busses via DS2484 device using the I²C Bus for communication.

components.name: A01NYUB Waterproof Ultrasonic Sensor
components.description: This sensor allows you to use A01NYUB waterproof ultrasonic sensor by DFRobot (datasheet) with ESPHome to measure distances. This sensor can measure ranges between 28 centimeters and 750 centimeters with a resolution of 1 millimeter.

components.name: A02YYUW Waterproof Ultrasonic Sensor
components.description: This sensor allows you to use A02YYUW waterproof ultrasonic sensor by DFRobot (datasheet) with ESPHome to measure distances. This sensor can measure ranges between 3 centimeters and 450 centimeters with a resolution of 1 millimeter.

components.name: Absolute Humidity
components.description: The absolute_humidity platform allows you to calculate absolute humidity from air temperature and relative humidity.

components.name: AC Dimmer Component
components.description: The ac_dimmer component allows you to connect a dimmable light or other load which supports phase control dimming to your ESPHome project.

components.name: ADC128S102 8-Channel 12-Bit A/D Converter
components.description: The ADC128S102 is a low-power, eight-channel CMOS 12-bit analog-to-digital converter specified for conversion throughput rates of 500 ksps to 1 MSPS. The converter is based on a successive-approximation register architecture with an internal track-and-hold circuit.

components.name: Addressable Light
components.description: The addressable_light display platform allows to display text and graphics on an addressable light that has been arranged in a display matrix.

components.name: ADE7880 Power Sensor
components.description: The ade7880 sensor platform allows you to use ADE7880 voltage/current/power sensors (datasheet) with ESPHome. This sensor chip is commonly found in Shelly 3EM and 3EM Pro devices.

components.name: ADE7953 Power Sensor
components.description: > [!NOTE] > This page is incomplete and could use some work. If you want to contribute, please see our > developer site. This page is missing: > > - A complete configuration example for the Shelly 2.5 > - An image for the front page The ade7953 sensor platform allows you to use ADE7953 single phase energy metering ICs (datasheet) with ESPHome. These are commonly found in Shelly devices.

components.name: ADE7953 Power Sensor
components.description: > [!NOTE] > This page is incomplete and could use some work. If you want to contribute, please see our > developer site. This page is missing: > > - A complete configuration example for the Shelly 2.5 > - An image for the front page The ade7953 sensor platform allows you to use ADE7953 single phase energy metering ICs (datasheet) with ESPHome. These are commonly found in Shelly devices.

components.name: ADS1115 4-Channel 16-Bit A/D Converter
components.description: The ads1115 domain creates a global hub so that you can later create individual sensors using the ADS1115 Sensor Platform. To use this hub, first setup the I²C Bus and connect the sensor to the pins specified there.

components.name: ADS1118 4-Channel 16-Bit A/D Converter with Internal Temperature Sensor
components.description: ADS1118 4-Channel 16-Bit A/D Converter (datasheet) The ads1118 domain creates a global hub so that you can later create individual sensors using the ADS1118 Sensor Platform. It uses the SPI Bus for communication.

components.name: AGS10 Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) Sensor
components.description: The ags10 sensor platform VOC sensor allows you to use your ASAIR AGS10 (datasheet, ASAIR) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: AHT10 Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The aht10 Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your AHT10 (datasheet), AHT20 (datasheet) or AHT30 (datasheet) I²C-based sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: AIC3204
components.description: The aic3204 platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the AIC3204 ultra low power stereo audio codec (DAC). This allows the playback of audio via the microcontroller from a range of sources via Media Player.

components.name: Air Quality Index (AQI)
components.description: The aqi sensor platform allows you to compute an Air Quality Index from PM2.5 and PM10 particulate matter sensor readings. This sensor works with any PM sensor source, such as Pmsx003, Hm3301, Sds011, or Sps30.

components.name: AirThings BLE Sensors
components.description: The airthingswaveplus and airthingswavemini sensor platforms lets you track the output of AirThings Bluetooth Low Energy devices.

components.name: AirTouch AT581x Radar
components.description: The AirTouch AT581x radar (aka AT581x ) is a family of 5.8GHz radar which can be used for human presence detection. It can detect tiny movements and compared to a PIR sensor it can detect presence continuously. This can be useful, for example, to turn the lights on when you enter a room, keep them on as long as you are there (without waving your hands at the sensor) and turn them off almost immedi.

components.name: AM2315C Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The am2315c Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your AM2315C (datasheet) I²C-based sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: AM2320 Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The am2320 Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your AM2320 (datasheet) I²C-based sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: AM43 Cover
components.description: Setting up AM43/BLE covers in ESPHome.

components.name: AMS AS3935 Franklin Lightning Sensor
components.description: The AS3935 sensor platform allows you to use your AS3935 sensor (datasheet) in order to get notified when a thunderstorm is getting close.

components.name: AMS AS3935 Franklin Lightning Sensor
components.description: The AS3935 sensor platform allows you to use your AS3935 sensor (datasheet) in order to get notified when a thunderstorm is getting close.

components.name: AMS AS3935 Franklin Lightning Sensor
components.description: The AS3935 sensor platform allows you to use your AS3935 sensor (datasheet) in order to get notified when a thunderstorm is getting close.

components.name: AMS iAQ-Core Indoor Air Quality Sensor
components.description: The AMS iAQ-Core sensor allows you to use your (datasheet) sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: Analog Devices MAX17043 battery fuel gauge
components.description: The MAX17043 platform allows you to use a MAX17043 to more accurately monitor the remaining capacity of a LIPO battery (datasheet, DFRobot) in ESPHome. It uses the I²C Bus for communication (the address is fixed at 0x36).

components.name: Analog Threshold Binary Sensor
components.description: The analog_threshold binary sensor platform allows you to convert analog values (i.e. sensor readings) into boolean values, using a threshold as a reference. When the signal is above or equal to the threshold the binary sensor is true (this behavior can be changed by adding an invert filter).

components.name: Analog To Digital Sensor
components.description: The Analog To Digital (adc ) Sensor allows you to use the built-in ADC in your device to measure a voltage on certain pins.

components.name: Animation
components.description: Animation.

components.name: Anova Cooker
components.description: The anova climate platform creates a climate device which can be used to control a BLE Anova sous-vide stick. Whilst not strictly a climate device, the concept of keeping something to a set temperature applies equally here.

components.name: APDS9306 Sensor
components.description: The apds9306 sensor component allows you to use APDS9306 ambient light sensors (datasheet, Broadcom) with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: APDS9960 Sensor
components.description: The apds9960 sensor platform allows you to use your APDS9960 RGB and gesture sensors (datasheet, SparkFun) with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: AS5600 12-Bit Magnetic Position Sensor
components.description: The as5600 sensor platform you to use your AS5600 (datasheet, AMS) or AS5600L (datasheet, AMS) 12-bit magnetic position sensor with ESPHome. Individual sensors will be added using the AS5600 Sensor Platform. To use this hub, first setup the I²C Bus and connect the sensor to the pins specified there.

components.name: AS7341 Spectral Color Sensor
components.description: The as7341 sensor platform allows you to use your AS7341 spectral color sensor (datasheet, Adafruit) with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Atlas Scientific Peristaltic Pump
components.description: The ezo_pmp component allows you to use an Atlas Scientific Peristaltic Pump with ESPHome. Both the EZO-PMP (datasheet) and EZO-PMP-L (datasheet) are supported. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: ATM90E26 Power Sensor
components.description: The atm90e26 sensor platform allows you to use your ATM90E26 voltage/current and power sensors (datasheet) with ESPHome. This sensor is found in the DitroniX GTEM ESP32 energy meter and other devices.

components.name: ATM90E32 Power Sensor
components.description: The atm90e32 sensor platform allows you to use your ATM90E32 voltage/current and power sensors (datasheet) with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in CircuitSetup 2 and 6 channel energy meters and the Gelidus Research 2 channel power meter.

components.name: Audio ADC Core
components.description: The audio_adc component allows your ESPHome devices to use audio ADC hardware components, allowing the capture/recording of audio via the microcontroller from a range of sources.

components.name: Audio DAC Core
components.description: The audio_dac component allows your ESPHome devices to use audio DAC hardware components, allowing the playback of audio via the microcontroller from a range of sources via Media Player.

components.name: AXS15231 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The axs15231 touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the AXS15231 chip with ESPHome. An I²C bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: b-parasite
components.description: b-parasite is an open source soil moisture and ambient temperature/humidity/light sensor.

components.name: Bang Bang Climate Controller
components.description: The bang_bang climate platform allows you to regulate a value with a bang-bang controller (also called hysteresis controller).

components.name: BedJet
components.description: The bedjet component allows you to communicate with a BedJet V3 Climate Comfort Sleep System.

components.name: Beken SPI LED Strip
components.description: This is a component using the Beken SPI DMA interface to drive addressable LED strips.

components.name: Belling BL0906 Energy Monitor
components.description: The bl0906 sensor platform allows you to use your BL0906 voltage/current/power and energy sensors with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in Athom EM2 Energy Meter and Athom EM6 Energy Meter > [!NOTE] > The current ratio of the current CT clamp must be 2000:1 As the communication with the BL0906 done using UART, you need to have an UART bus in your configuration with the txpin and rxpin connec.

components.name: Belling BL0940 Energy Monitor
components.description: The bl0940 component allows you to use BL0940 energy monitors sensors with ESPHome. These are used in some Tuya-devices (e.g. the power metering BW-SHP10) and the Vaiotech Rowi2 Smart Plug.

components.name: Belling BL0942 Energy Monitor
components.description: The bl0942 sensor platform allows you to use BL0942 energy monitors sensors with ESPHome. These are used in some Tuya-devices (e.g. Aubess Tuya WiFi Mini Smart Switch With Energy Monitor) The communication with this component is done via a UART. You must therefore have a uart: entry in your configuration with both the TX and RX pins set to some pins on your board and the baud rate set to 4800 with.

components.name: BH1750 Ambient Light Sensor
components.description: The bh1750 sensor platform allows you to use your BH1750 (datasheet) ambient light sensor with ESPHome. The I²C bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BH1900NUX Temperature Sensor
components.description: The bh1900nux sensor platform allows you to use the BH1900NUX (datasheet) temperature sensor from Rohm Semiconductor with ESPHome. The I²C bus must be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Binary Fan
components.description: The binary fan platform lets you represent any binary Output Component as a fan.

components.name: BL0939 Power Sensor
components.description: The bl0939 sensor platform allows you to use your BL0939 voltage/current/power and energy sensors with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in Sonoff Dual R3 v2.

components.name: BLE Client
components.description: Configuration of the BLE client on ESP32.

components.name: BLE Component
components.description: The esp32_ble component in ESPHome sets up the Bluetooth LE stack on the device so that a Esp32 Ble Server can run.

components.name: BLE Server
components.description: The esp32bleserver component in ESPHome sets up a BLE GATT server that exposes the device name, manufacturer and board. BLE GATT services and characteristics can be added to the server to expose data and control.

components.name: Bluetooth Proxy
components.description: Home Assistant can expand its Bluetooth reach by communicating through the Bluetooth proxy component in ESPHome. The individual device integrations in Home Assistant (such as BTHome) will receive the data from the Bluetooth Integration in Home Assistant which automatically aggregates all ESPHome Bluetooth proxies with any USB Bluetooth Adapters you might have. This exceptional feature offers fault.

components.name: BM8563 Time Source
components.description: BM8563 Time Source.

components.name: BME280 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The bme280 sensor platform allows you to use your BME280 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature, pressure and humidity sensors with ESPHome. The sensor is used in Forced Mode where measurement is performed and then the sensor returns to sleep mode until next measurement. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BME280 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The bme280 sensor platform allows you to use your BME280 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature, pressure and humidity sensors with ESPHome. The sensor is used in Forced Mode where measurement is performed and then the sensor returns to sleep mode until next measurement. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BME680 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity+Gas Sensor
components.description: The bme680 sensor platform allows you to use your BME680 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature, pressure and humidity sensors with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BME680 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity+Gas Sensor via BSEC
components.description: The bme680_bsec sensor platform allows you to use your BME680 (datasheet, Adafruit, Pimoroni) temperature, pressure and humidity and gas sensors with ESPHome via the Bosch Sensortec Environmental Cluster (BSEC) software library. The use of Bosch's proprietary algorithms provide an Index for Air Quality (IAQ) measurement derived from the gas resistance sensor's response to specific Volatile Organic.

components.name: BME68x Temperature, Humidity, Pressure & Gas Sensor via BSEC2
components.description: The bme68xbsec2i2c sensor platform allows you to use your BME680 and BME688 (Adafruit, Pimoroni) temperature, humidity, pressure and gas sensors with ESPHome via the Bosch Sensortec Environmental Cluster 2 (BSEC2) software library. The use of Bosch's proprietary algorithms provide an Index for Air Quality (IAQ) measurement derived from the gas resistance sensor's response to specific Volatile Orga.

components.name: BME68x Temperature, Humidity, Pressure & Gas Sensor via BSEC2
components.description: The bme68xbsec2i2c sensor platform allows you to use your BME680 and BME688 (Adafruit, Pimoroni) temperature, humidity, pressure and gas sensors with ESPHome via the Bosch Sensortec Environmental Cluster 2 (BSEC2) software library. The use of Bosch's proprietary algorithms provide an Index for Air Quality (IAQ) measurement derived from the gas resistance sensor's response to specific Volatile Orga.

components.name: BMI160 Accelerometer/Gyroscope Sensor
components.description: The bmi160 sensor platform allows you to use your BMI160 Accelerometer/Gyroscope (datasheet, SparkFun) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP085 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The BMP085 sensor platform allows you to use your BMP085 (datasheet, Adafruit) and BMP180 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP280 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp280 sensor platform allows you to use your BMP280 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP280 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp280 sensor platform allows you to use your BMP280 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP388 / BMP390 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp3xx sensor platform allows you to use your BMP388 or BMP390 (datasheet, BMP390 product page) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. Either I²C bus or SPI bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP388 / BMP390 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp3xx sensor platform allows you to use your BMP388 or BMP390 (datasheet, BMP390 product page) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. Either I²C bus or SPI bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP581 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp581 sensor platform allows you to use your BMP581 (datasheet, SparkFun or Adafruit) and BMP585 (datasheet) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. Either an I²C bus or an SPI bus are required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BP1658CJ LED driver
components.description: The BP1658CJ component represents a BP1658CJ LED driver chain in ESPHome. Communication is done with two GPIO pins (DATA and CLK).

components.name: BP5758D LED driver
components.description: The BP5758D component represents a BP5758D LED driver chain in ESPHome. Communication is done with two GPIO pins (DATA and CLK).

components.name: CAP1188 Capacitive Touch Sensor
components.description: The cap1188 sensor platform allows you to use your CAP1188 (datasheet, Adafruit) Capacitive Touch Sensor with ESPHome. The I²C bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Captive Portal
components.description: The captive portal component in ESPHome is a fallback mechanism for when connecting to the configured WiFi fails.

components.name: CC1101 Low-Power Sub-1 GHz RF Transceiver
components.description: The CC1101 component provides a driver for the Texas Instruments CC1101 Sub-1 GHz RF Transceiver (datasheet). The CC1101 is a low-cost, low-power radio chip commonly used for wireless communication in the 300-928 MHz frequency bands, including the popular 315 MHz, 433 MHz, 868 MHz, and 915 MHz ISM bands.

components.name: CCS811 eCO_2 and Volatile Organic Compound Sensor
components.description: The ccs811 sensor platform allows you to use CCS811 eCO_2 and volatile organic compound sensors (Adafruit) with ESPHome.

components.name: cd74hc4067 Analog Multiplexer
components.description: The cd74hc4067 Analog Multiplexer component allows you to attach up to 16 analog input sensors to a single ADC pin. The component is used to activate the right multiplexer pin for each of the defined cd74hc4067 sensors.

components.name: CFSensor XGZP68xx Non-C Series Differential Pressure Sensor
components.description: CFSensor makes multiple generations of sensors with identical model numbers such as the 6899D or 6897D, but unfortunately with completely different I²C interfaces. You can identify which from the part number: Another way of telling the difference is the I²C address: Unfortunately CFSensor have removed from their website the datasheets for the non-C series of sensors. You will need to specifically.

components.name: CH422G I/O Expander
components.description: The CH422G component allows you to use the CH422G I/O expander in ESPHome. It uses an I²C Bus for communication. The I²C address is not configurable as the CH422G has a separate address for each internal register.

components.name: CH423 I/O Expander
components.description: The CH423 component allows you to use the CH423 I/O expander in ESPHome. It uses an I²C Bus for communication. The I²C address is not configurable as the CH423 has a separate address for each internal register.

components.name: chsc6x Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The chsc6x touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the chsc6x chip with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: Cold White + Warm White Light
components.description: The cwww light platform creates a cold white + warm white light from 2 float output components (one for each channel). The two channels can be controlled individually or together.

components.name: Color Temperature Light
components.description: The color_temperature light platform creates a Color Temperature light from 2 float output components. One channel controls the LED temperature, and the other channel controls the brightness.

components.name: Copy Component
components.description: The copy component can be used to copy an existing component (like a sensor, switch, etc.) and create a duplicate mirroring the source's state and forwarding actions such as turning on to the source.

components.name: CS5460A Power Sensor
components.description: CS5460A component configuration.

components.name: CSE7761 Power Sensor
components.description: The cse7761 sensor platform allows you to use your CSE7761 voltage/current and power sensors with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in Sonoff Dual R3 v1.x.

components.name: CSE7766 Power Sensor
components.description: The cse7766 sensor platform allows you to use your CSE7766 voltage/current and power sensors (datasheet) sensors with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in Sonoff POW R2. CSE7759B is similar to CSE7766 and works with this component.

components.name: cst226 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The cst226 touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the cst226 chip with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: CT Clamp Current Sensor
components.description: The Current Transformer Clamp (ct_clamp ) sensor allows you to hook up a CT Clamp to an analog voltage sensor (like the ADC sensor) and convert the readings to measured single phase AC current.

components.name: CUBIC CM1106 Single Beam NDIR CO2 Sensor Module
components.description: The cm1106 sensor platform allows you to use CM1106 CO2 sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: CUBIC PM2005/PM2105 Laser Particle Sensor Module
components.description: The pm2005 sensor platform allows you to use your PM2005/PM2105 laser particulate matter sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: Current Based Cover
components.description: The current_based cover platform allows you to create covers with position control by using current sensors to detect the fully-open and fully-closed states. This is pretty useful when using motors with integrated mechanical endstops. During cover operation, the component monitors the current consumption to detect when the motor has stopped.

components.name: DAC7678
components.description: The DAC7678 component represents a DAC7678 12-bit 8 channel DAC with internal reference (datasheet) in ESPHome. It uses the I²C Bus for communication.

components.name: Dallas Temperature Sensor
components.description: The dallas_temp component allows you to use DS18B20 (datasheet) and similar 1-Wire temperature sensors. A 1-Wire bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Daly BMS Sensor
components.description: The daly_bms sensor platform allows you to use a Daly Smart BMS (more info) with ESPHome.

components.name: Debug Component
components.description: The debug component can be used to debug problems with ESPHome. At startup, it prints a bunch of useful information like reset reason, free heap size, ESPHome version and so on. It also allows you to get the same information as a text sensor, and to monitor the state of the ESP heap memory (free space, maximum free block size and fragmentation level) and the main-loop timing. Monitoring can be don.

components.name: Deep Sleep Component
components.description: The deep_sleep component can be used to automatically enter a deep sleep mode on the ESP8266/ESP32/BK72xx after a certain amount of time. This is especially useful with nodes that operate on batteries and therefore need to conserve as much energy as possible.

components.name: Dew Point
components.description: The dew_point platform allows you to calculate the dew point from air temperature and relative humidity. For the calculation the Magnus formula approximation is used.

components.name: DF-Player mini
components.description: The dfplayer (datasheet), component allows you to play sound and music stored in an SD card or USB flash drive.

components.name: DFRobot mmWave Radar
components.description: The DFRobot mmWave Radar (aka SEN0395 ) is a 24GHz radar which can be used for human presence detection. It can detect tiny movements and compared to a PIR sensor it can detect presence continuously. This can be useful, for example, to turn the lights on when you enter a room, keep them on as long as you are there (without waving your hands at the sensor) and turn them off almost immediately after.

components.name: DHT Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The DHT Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: DHT12 Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The dht12 Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your DHT12 (datasheet, electrodragon) I²C-based sensor with ESPHome. This sensor is also called AM2320 by some sellers.

components.name: DLMS Meter
components.description: The dlms_meter component connects to smart meters which use the encrypted DLMS/COSEM protocol over UART. These smart meters are (for example) widely deployed in Austria.

components.name: DPS310/DPS368 Atmospheric Pressure Sensor
components.description: The dps310 sensor platform allows you to use both the temperature and pressure sensors on your DPS310 or DPS368 atmospheric pressure sensor (Adafruit) with ESPHome. The I²C component is required to be set up in your configuration.

components.name: DS1307 Time Source
components.description: DS1307 Time Source.

components.name: DSMR Component
components.description: The DSMR component connects to Dutch Smart Meters which comply to DSMR (Dutch Smart Meter Requirements), also known as 'Slimme meter' or 'P1 port'.

components.name: Duty Cycle Sensor
components.description: The duty cycle sensor allows you to measure for what percentage of time a signal on a GPIO pin is HIGH or LOW.

components.name: Duty Time
components.description: The dutytime sensor allows you to track the total duty time of some object, for example, a light bulb, in seconds. Able to calculate the last turn-on time when an optional sensor lasttime is included in the configuration.

components.name: Eastron SDM Energy Monitor
components.description: The sdm_meter sensor platform allows you to use Eastron SDM modbus energy monitors (website) with ESPHome.

components.name: EE895 CO₂, Temperature and Pressure Sensor
components.description: The ee895 sensor platform allows you to use your EE895 CO₂ (datasheet, E+E EE895) sensor with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work. We recommend the use of pull up resistors.

components.name: EKTF2232 Touchscreen Controller
components.description: The ektf2232 component allows using the touchscreen controller found in the Inkplate 6 Plus with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: EMC2101 Fan Controller and Temperature sensor
components.description: The emc2101 component allows you to control a cooling fan from ESPHome (datasheet). It also provides temperature sensors and a tachometer. The fan can be controlled either by a PWM signal or by outputing a DC voltage using its DAC.

components.name: Endstop Cover
components.description: The endstop cover platform allows you to create covers with position control that have endstops at both ends of the cover to detect the fully-open and fully-closed states. When any of these endstops are reached, the cover is stopped (via stop_action ) and the corresponding state is sent out.

components.name: ENS160 Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) and eCO₂ Sensor
components.description: The ens160 sensor platform allows you to use your ENS160 (datasheet) air-quality sensors with ESPHome. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: ENS160 Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) and eCO₂ Sensor
components.description: The ens160 sensor platform allows you to use your ENS160 (datasheet) air-quality sensors with ESPHome. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: ENS210 Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The ENS210 Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your ENS210 (datasheet) i2c-based sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: ES7210
components.description: The es7210 platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the ES7210 high performance four channel audio ADC (datasheet). This allows attached microphones to be used as a microphone input via I2S Audio.

components.name: ES7243E
components.description: The es7243e platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the ES7243E high performance four channel audio ADC (datasheet). This allows attached microphones to be used as a microphone input via I2S Audio.

components.name: ES8156
components.description: The es8156 platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the ES8156 low power mono audio codec. This allows the playback of audio via the microcontroller from a range of sources via Speaker or Media Player.

components.name: ES8311
components.description: The es8311 platform allows your ESPHome devices to use the ES8311 low power mono audio codec. This allows the playback of audio via the microcontroller from a range of sources via Speaker or Media Player.

components.name: ES8388
components.description: The es8388 component allows your ESPHome devices to use the es8388 low power audio codec (datasheet) This allows the playback of audio through two channel high performance audio DAC output via the microcontroller from a range of sources via Speaker or Media Player.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy Beacon
components.description: The esp32blebeacon component creates a Bluetooth Low Energy Beacon with your ESP32 device. Beacons are BLE devices that repeatedly just send out a pre-defined packet of data. This packet can then be received by devices like smartphones and can then be used to track a phone's location.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy Device
components.description: The ble_presence binary sensor platform lets you track the presence of a Bluetooth Low Energy device.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy RSSI Sensor
components.description: The ble_rssi sensor platform lets you track the RSSI value or signal strength of a BLE device. See the binary sensor setup for instructions for setting up this platform.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy Scanner
components.description: The ble_scanner text sensor platform lets you track reachable BLE devices.

components.name: ESP32 Bluetooth Low Energy Tracker Hub
components.description: The esp32bletracker component creates a global hub so that you can track bluetooth low energy devices using your ESP32 node.

components.name: ESP32 Camera Component
components.description: The esp32_camera component allows you to use ESP32-based camera boards in ESPHome that directly integrate into Home Assistant through the native API.

components.name: ESP32 Camera Web Server Component
components.description: The esp32cameraweb_server component allows you to use expose web server of ESP32-based camera boards in ESPHome that directly can be integrated into external surveillance or PVR software.

components.name: ESP32 CAN
components.description: The ESP32 has an integrated CAN controller and therefore doesn't necessarily need an external controller. Some variants (ESP32-C5, ESP32-C6, ESP32-P4) have multiple CAN controllers - see Multiple CAN Controllers below. You only need to specify the RX and TX pins. Any GPIO will work.

components.name: ESP32 DAC
components.description: The ESP32 DAC platform allows you to output analog voltages using the 8-bit digital-to-analog converter of the ESP32. Unlike the Ledc, which can simulate an analog signal by using a fast switching frequency, the hardware DAC can output a real analog signal with no need for additional filtering.

components.name: ESP32 Hosted
components.description: ESP32 Hosted (ESP-Hosted-MCU) is a solution that allows you to use ESP32 modules as communication co-processors. This solution provides wireless connectivity (Wi-Fi and Bluetooth) to the host module, enabling it to communicate with other devices.

components.name: ESP32 LEDC Output
components.description: The LEDC output component exposes a LEDC PWMchannel of the ESP32 as an output component.

components.name: ESP32 Platform
components.description: Configuration for the ESP32 platform for ESPHome.

components.name: ESP32 RMT LED Strip
components.description: This is a component using the ESP32 RMT peripheral to drive most addressable LED strips.

components.name: ESP32 Touch Pad
components.description: Capacitive touch detection is possible on ESP32, ESP32-S2, ESP32-S3 and ESP32-P4 processors. In ESPHome, it is configured in two parts: The esp32_touch component creates a global hub enabling (capacitive) touch detection on GPIO pins supported by ESP32, ESP32-S2, ESP32-S3 and ESP32-P4 processors. With this enabled, binary sensors may then be configured to permit touch detection.

components.name: ESP8266 Platform
components.description: Configuration for the ESP8266 platform for ESPHome.

components.name: ESP8266 Software PWM Output
components.description: The ESP8266 Software PWM platform allows you to use a software PWM on the pins GPIO0-GPIO16 on your ESP8266. Note that this is a software PWM, so there can be some flickering during periods of high WiFi activity. Hardware PWMs like the one on the ESP32 (see Ledc) are preferred.

components.name: ESPHome Core Configuration
components.description: Here you specify some core information that ESPHome needs to create firmwares. Most importantly, this is the section of the configuration where you specify the name of the node.

components.name: ESPNow communication Component
components.description: The ESPNow component allows ESPHome to communicate with esp32 devices in a simple and unrestricted way. It enables the option to interact with other esp32 devices over the Espressif's ESP-NOW protocol, see documentation. It can be used with the Packet Transport Component to broadcast sensor data, see ESP-NOW Packet Transport Platform.

components.name: EZO sensor circuits
components.description: The ezo sensor platform allows you to use your EZO sensor circuits with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work. All embedded solutions from EZO can be found on their website. If a certain command is not supported directly, it can be executed with the send_custom() method call.

components.name: Factory Reset
components.description: The factory_reset component allows you to invalidate (reset) all ESPHome preferences stored in flash memory and reboot your node. After reboot all states, parameters and variables will be reinitialized with their default values. This is useful: > [!NOTE] > USE WITH GREAT CAUTION! All credentials, global variables, counters and saved states stored in non-volatile memory will be lost with no chance.

components.name: FastLED Light
components.description: > [!WARNING] > FastLED does not work as expected with Arduino 3 or newer for ESP8266. For now, you can either downgrade the arduino version or use Neopixelbus. > > ``yaml > esp8266: > framework: > version: 2.7.4 > `` > > See these related issues: > > - FastLED/FastLED#1322 > - FastLED/FastLED#1264 > [!WARNING] > FastLED does not work with ESP-IDF. > > For addressable lights, you can use Esp32 Rmt.

components.name: Feedback Cover
components.description: The feedback cover platform allows you to create covers with position control that can optionally have feedback from sensors to detect the fully-open and fully-closed states (endstops), and from sensors to detect actual movement (opening/closing). In all the cases the current position is approximated with the time the cover has been moving in a direction.

components.name: Font Renderer Component
components.description: ESPHome's graphical rendering engine also has a powerful font drawer which integrates seamlessly into the system. You have the option to use any OpenType/TrueType (.ttf, .otf, .woff ) font file at any size, as well as fixed-size PCF and BDF bitmap fonts.

components.name: FS3000 Air Velocity Sensor
components.description: The FS3000 Air Velocity sensor allows you to use your FS3000 (datasheet, sparkfun) sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: FT5X06 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The ft5x06 touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the ft5x06 chip with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: FT63X6 Touchscreen Controller
components.description: The ft63x6 component allows using the touchscreen controller found in Seeed Studio's WT32-SC01 with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: FTLab GDK101 Gamma Radiation Sensor Module
components.description: The GDK101 sensor platform allows you to use your GDK101 sensor to monitor radiation levels at your place.

components.name: Generic I²C device component
components.description: Generic I²C device component.

components.name: GL-R01 I²C - Time Of Flight Mini LiDAR Laser Ranging Sensor
components.description: GL-R01 I²C - Time Of Flight Mini LiDAR Laser Ranging Sensor.

components.name: GP8403 Component
components.description: The `gp8403 (12-bit) and gp8413` (15-bit) are 2-channel DAC output modules respectively. They require an I2C to be setup.

components.name: GPIO Binary Sensor
components.description: The GPIO Binary Sensor platform allows you to use any input pin on your device as a binary sensor. By default, it uses hardware interrupts for efficient state change detection with minimal CPU usage.

components.name: GPS Component
components.description: The gps component allows you to connect GPS modules to your ESPHome project. Any GPS module that uses the standardized NMEA communication protocol will work.

components.name: Graph Component
components.description: You can display a graph of a sensor value(s) using this component. The states used for the graph are stored in memory at the time the sensor updates and will be lost when the device reboots.

components.name: Graphical Display Menu
components.description: The component provides an infrastructure for setting up a hierarchical menu on graphical displays. This offers the user an interactive method to display labels, control entities like switch, select, number available locally on the ESPHome node, without the requirement of a network connection.

components.name: Grove Multichannel Gas Sensor V2
components.description: The grovegasmc_v2 sensor platform allows you to use your Grove Multichannel GasSensor V2 with ESPHome. It exposes 4 different gas sensors for qualitatively measuring Nitrogen Dioxide (NO2), Carbon Monoxide (CO), Ethanol (C2H5OH), and Volatile Organic Compounds (VOCs).

components.name: Grove TB6612FNG Motor Drive
components.description: The Grove TBB6612FNG a runs over I²C bus and has the capability to control DC and Stepper motors. At the current stage of implementation only DC motor is implemented.

components.name: Grow Fingerprint Reader
components.description: The fingerprint_grow component allows you to use your R307, R503, R503-RGB, ZFM-20, ... fingerprint sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: Growatt Solar
components.description: The Growatt Inverter sensor platform allows you to use growatt inverter data reading on modbus with ESPHome.

components.name: Grundfos Alpha3
components.description: The alpha3 component allows you to monitor a Grundfos Alpha3 Model B circulator pump over BLE.

components.name: gt911 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The gt911 touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the gt911 chip with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: H-bridge Fan
components.description: The hbridge fan platform allows you to use a compatible h-bridge (L298N, DRV8871, MX1508, BTS7960, L9110S, DRV8833, TB6612, etc.) to control a fan (or motor/solenoid).

components.name: Haier Climate Binary Sensors
components.description: Additional sensors for Haier Climate device. These sensors are supported only by the hOn protocol.

components.name: Havells Solar
components.description: The Havells Inverter sensor platform allows you to use Havells inverter data reading on modbus (website) with ESPHome.

components.name: HC8 CO₂ Sensor
components.description: The hc8 sensor platform allows you to use HC8 CO₂ sensors.

components.name: HDC1080 Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The HDC1080 Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your HDC1080 (datasheet, Adafruit) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: HDC2010 High Precision Temperature and Humidity Sensor
components.description: The HDC2010 Temperature and Humidity sensor allows you to use your HDC2010 (datasheet, Itbrainpower) sensors with ESPHome. The I2C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: HDC302x Temperature and Humidity Sensor
components.description: The hdc302x sensor platform allows you to use your HDC3020, HDC3021, or HDC3022 (datasheet, Users Guide, Adafruit) sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: HE60R Garage Door Opener
components.description: Setting up a Chamberlain HomEntry HE60R and similar garage door openers.

components.name: HHCCJCY01 Moved To Xiaomi BLE
components.description: HHCCJCY01 Moved To Xiaomi BLE.

components.name: HHCCJCY10 Xiaomi MiFlora (Pink version)
components.description: MiFlora, tuya (pink) version, measures temperature, moisture, ambient light and nutrient levels in the soil.

components.name: HLK-FM22x Face Recognition Module
components.description: The hlk_fm22x component allows you to use your HLK-FM225 and HLK-FM223 face recognition modules with ESPHome.

components.name: HLW8012 Power Sensor
components.description: The hlw8012 sensor platform allows you to use your HLW8012 voltage/current and power sensors (datasheet) sensors with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in Sonoff POWs. CSE7759 and BL0937 are similar to HLW8012 and work with this component. Beware that CSE7759B is different and should be used with the CSE7766 component.

components.name: HLW8032 Power Sensor
components.description: The hlw8032 sensor platform allows you to use your HLW8032 voltage/current and power sensors (datasheet) with ESPHome. This sensor is commonly found in M5Stack modules.

components.name: HMC5883L Magnetometer
components.description: The hmc5883l allows you to use your HMC5883L triple-axis magnetometers (datasheet, Adafruit) with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Home Assistant Binary Sensor
components.description: The homeassistant binary sensor platform allows you to create binary sensors that import states from your Home Assistant instance using the native API.

components.name: Honeywell ABP 2 Pressure Sensors
components.description: The honeywellabp2_i2c sensor platform allows you to use your Honeywell ABP2 (datasheet) pressure and temperature sensors with ESPHome. The I2C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work src="/images/honeywellabp.jpg" alt="" caption="Honeywell ABP Pressure and Temperature Sensor." layout="constrained" width={300} height={261} /> The values for minpressure and maxpressure.

components.name: Honeywell ABP Pressure Sensors
components.description: The honeywellabp sensor platform allows you to use your Honeywell ABP (datasheet, Mouser) pressure and temperature sensors with ESPHome. The SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work src="/images/honeywellabp.jpg" alt="" caption="Honeywell ABP Pressure and Temperature Sensor." layout="constrained" width={300} height={261} /> The values for minpressure and maxpressu.

components.name: Honeywell HumidIcon (I2C HIH series) Temperature & Humidity Sensor
components.description: Honeywell HumidIcon (I2C HIH series) Temperature & Humidity sensors with ESPHome (datasheet). The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Host Platform
components.description: Configuration for the host platform for ESPHome.

components.name: HRXL/XL MaxSonar WR Series
components.description: This sensor allows you to use HRXL MaxSonar WR series ultrasonic sensors by MaxBotix (datasheet) or the XL MaxSonar WR series (datasheet) with ESPHome to measure distances. Depending on the model, these sensors can measure in a range between 30 centimeters and 10 meters.

components.name: HTE501 Temperature+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The hte501 sensor platform Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your HTE501 (datasheet, E+E HTE501) sensor with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: HTTP Request
components.description: The http_request component lets you make HTTP/HTTPS requests. To do so, you need to add it to your device's configuration: ESPHome/ (https://esphome.io) where is the version of ESPHome the device is running. For example: ESPHome/2024.6.0 (https://esphome.io) connection; if invalid, the connection will be aborted. To accomplish this, ESP-IDF's default ESP x509 certificate bundle is included in the.

components.name: HTU21D | Si7021 | SHT21 Temperature & Humidity Sensor
components.description: The HTU21D Temperature & Humidity component allows you to use HTU21D, Si7021 and SHT21 sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: HTU31D Temperature & Humidity Sensor
components.description: The HTU31D Temperature & Humidity component allows you to use HTU31D sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: HUB75 RGB LED Matrix Display
components.description: The hub75 display platform allows you to use HUB75 RGB LED matrix panels with ESPHome. This component uses a high-performance DMA-based driver that provides efficient, low-CPU-overhead driving of LED matrix panels.

components.name: HX711 Load Cell Amplifier
components.description: The hx711 sensor platform allows you to use your HX711 load cell amplifier (datasheet, SparkFun) with ESPHome src={hx711FullImg} alt="" caption="HX711 Load Cell Amplifier. Image by SparkFun licensed and re-distributed under CC BY 2.0." layout="constrained" /> Connect GND to GND, VCC to 3.3V and the other two DOUT (or DT for short) and CLK (or SCK ) to free GPIO pins.

components.name: Hydreon Rain Sensor Binary Sensor
components.description: The hydreon_rgxx binary sensor platform gives access to information provided by a Hydreon Rain Sensor. For this sensor to work, a Hydreon Rgxx must be set up.

components.name: HYT271 Temperature & Humidity Sensor
components.description: The HYT271 Temperature & Humidity sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: ILI9xxx TFT LCD Series
components.description: With this display driver you can control the following displays: More display drivers will come in the future.

components.name: Improv via BLE
components.description: The esp32_improv component in ESPHome implements the open Improv standard for configuring Wi-Fi on an ESP32 device by using Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) to receive the credentials.

components.name: INA219 DC Current Sensor
components.description: The ina219 sensor platform allows you to use your INA219 High Side DC Current Sensor (datasheet, Adafruit) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: INA226 DC current and power sensor
components.description: The ina226 sensor platform allows you to use your INA226 DC Current and Power Sensor (datasheet, eBay) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: INA260 DC Current and Power sensor
components.description: The ina260 sensor platform allows you to use your INA260 DC Current and Power Sensor (datasheet, Adafruit) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: INA2xx family of digital power monitors
components.description: src={ina228FullImg} alt="" caption="INA228 Module with integrated shunt from Adafruit" layout="constrained" /> The ina2xx sensor platform allows you to use family of Texas Instruments current and power sensors with ESPHome. Appropriate interface (I²C Bus or SPI Bus) is requred to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: INA2xx family of digital power monitors
components.description: src={ina228FullImg} alt="" caption="INA228 Module with integrated shunt from Adafruit" layout="constrained" /> The ina2xx sensor platform allows you to use family of Texas Instruments current and power sensors with ESPHome. Appropriate interface (I²C Bus or SPI Bus) is requred to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: INA3221 3-Channel DC Current Sensor
components.description: The ina3221 sensor platform allows you to use your INA3221 3-Channel DC Current Sensor (datasheet, switchdoc) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Inkbird IBS-TH1, IBS-TH1 Mini, and IBS-TH2 BLE Sensor
components.description: The inkbirdibsth1mini sensor platform lets you track the output of Inkbird IBS-TH1, IBS-TH1 Mini, and IBS-TH2 Bluetooth Low Energy devices using the Esp32 Ble Tracker. This component will track the temperature, external temperature (non mini only), humidity and the battery level of the IBS-TH1 device every time the sensor sends out a BLE broadcast. Note that contrary to other implementations, ESPH.

components.name: Inkplate 5, 6, 10 and 6 Plus
components.description: All-in-one e-paper display Inkplate 5, Inkplate 6, Inkplate 10 and Inkplate 6 Plus.

components.name: Integration Sensor
components.description: The integration sensor is a helper sensor that can integrate values from other sensors over time. This can for example be useful to integrate the values of a water flow sensor (in m^3/s) over time (result is in m^3).

components.name: Internal Temperature Sensor
components.description: The internal_temperature sensor platform allows you to use the integrated temperature sensor of the ESP32, RP2040, BK72XX, nRF52, and LN882H chips.

components.name: IR/RF Proxy
components.description: > [!IMPORTANT] > This component is EXPERIMENTAL. The API may change at any time > without following the normal breaking changes policy. Use at your own risk. > Once the API is considered stable, this warning will be removed.

components.name: I²C Bus
components.description: This component sets up the I²C bus for your ESP32, ESP8266, RP2040 or NRF52. In order for these components to work correctly, you need to define the I²C bus in your configuration. On ESP32 (both Arduino and ESP-IDF frameworks), internal pullup resistors are enabled by default. On ESP8266, the internal 10kΩ pullup resistors are always enabled. External pullups are still recommended for longer wire.

components.name: I²S Audio Component
components.description: The i2s_audio component allows for sending and receiving audio via I²S. This component only works on ESP32 based chips.

components.name: JSN-SR04T Waterproof Ultrasonic Range Finder
components.description: This sensor allows you to use the JSN-SR04T and AJ_SR04M Waterproof Ultrasonic Range Finder in Mode 1 and 2 with ESPHome to measure distances. This sensor can measure ranges between 25 centimeters and 600 centimeters with a resolution of 1 millimeter.

components.name: Kamstrup Meter Protocol [KMP]
components.description: Kamstrup Meter Protocol [KMP].

components.name: Key collector component
components.description: Key collector component.

components.name: Kuntze pool monitor
components.description: The kuntze component allows you to integrate the Kuntze water measurement instrument in ESPHome. It uses UART (ModBUS) for communication.

components.name: LC709203F Battery Monitor
components.description: The lc709203f sensor platform allows you to use a LC709203F (datasheet) battery monitor with ESPHome. This device is available as a standalone sensor and it is also one of the battery monitor chips used on the ESP32 Feather dev boards.

components.name: LCD Menu
components.description: The component provides an infrastructure for setting up a hierarchical menu on character based LCD displays. This offers the user an interactive method to display labels, control entities like switch, select, number available locally on the ESPHome node, without the requirement of a network connection.

components.name: LD2410 Sensor
components.description: The ld2410 sensor platform allows you to use HI-LINK LD2410 motion and presence sensors with ESPHome. There are three variants with similar communication protocols: The UART is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work, parity and stop_bits must be respectively NONE and 1.

components.name: LD2412 Sensor
components.description: The ld2412 sensor platform allows you to use HI-LINK LD2412 motion and presence sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: LD2420 24GHz mmWave Radar Sensor
components.description: The ld2420 sensor platform allows you to use the HLK-LD2420 motion and presence sensor. The UART is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: LD2450 Sensor
components.description: The ld2450 component allows you to use the HLK-LD2450 human presence, motion detection and tracking radar module with ESPHome.

components.name: LibreTiny PWM Output
components.description: The LibreTiny PWM platform allows you to use a hardware PWM on BK72xx and RTL87xx chips. Refer to LibreTiny/Boards to find your board and which PWM pins it supports.

components.name: Light Partition
components.description: The partition light platform allows you to combine multiple addressable light segments (like Fastled or Neopixelbus) and/or individual lights (like Rgb) into a single addressable light. This platform also allows splitting up an addressable light into multiple segments, so that segments can be individually controlled.

components.name: LightWaveRF
components.description: LightWaveRF Switch Lights.

components.name: Lilygo T5 4.7\" Touchscreen
components.description: The liygot547 touchscreen platform allows using the touchscreen controller for the Lilygo T5 4.7" e-Paper Display with ESPHome. The I²C component is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: Lite-On Ambient Light & Proximity Sensors
components.description: src={ltr501FullImg} alt="" caption="LTR-501 on a breadboard from Olimex" layout="constrained" /> src={ltr501UiImg} caption="LTR-501 Sensor in Home Assistant UI." layout="constrained" alt="" /> The ltr501 sensor platform allows you to use a range of LiteOn ambient light and proximity sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: Lite-On Ambient Light & Proximity Sensors
components.description: src={ltr303FullImg} alt="" caption="LTR-329 and LTR-303 Ambient Light Sensors." layout="constrained" /> src={ltr303UiImg} caption="LTR-303 Sensor in Home Assistant UI." layout="constrained" alt="" /> The ltralsps sensor platform allows you to use a range of LiteOn ambient light and proximity sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: LM75B Temperature Sensor
components.description: The LM75B Temperature sensor allows you to use your NXP Semiconductors LM75B (datasheet) sensor with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Logger Component
components.description: The logger component automatically logs all log messages through the serial port and through MQTT topics (if there is an MQTT client in the configuration). By default, all logs with a severity DEBUG or higher will be shown. Increasing the log level severity (to e.g INFO or WARN ) can help with the performance of the application and memory size.

components.name: LPS22 Barometric Pressure Sensor
components.description: The lps22 sensor platform allows you to use your LPS22HB, LPS22HH and LPS22DF pressure sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: LTR390 UV and Ambient Light Sensor
components.description: The ltr390 sensor platform allows you to use your LTR390 UV and ambient light sensor (datasheet, Adafruit) with ESPHome.

components.name: LYWSDCGQ Moved To Xiaomi BLE
components.description: LYWSDCGQ Moved To Xiaomi BLE.

components.name: M5Stack KMeterISO I2C K-Type probe temperature sensor
components.description: The kmeteriso sensor platform allows you to use your KMeterISO (product, M5Stack) K-Type thermocouple temperature sensor with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: M5Stack Unit 8 Angle
components.description: Setting up the M5Stack Unit 8 Angle input device with 8 knobs.

components.name: Matrix keypad
components.description: Matrix key input panel.

components.name: MAX31855 K-Type Thermocouple Temperature Sensor
components.description: The max31855 temperature sensor allows you to use your MAX31855 thermocouple temperature sensor (datasheet) with ESPHome As the communication with the MAX31855 is done using SPI, you need to have an SPI bus in your configuration with the **miso_pin** set (MOSI is not required).

components.name: MAX31856 Thermocouple Temperature Sensor
components.description: The MAX31856 temperature sensor allows you to use your MAX31856 Thermocouple temperature sensor (datasheet) with ESPHome.

components.name: MAX31865 Platinum RTD Temperature Sensor
components.description: The max31865 temperature sensor allows you to use your max31865 RTD temperature sensor (datasheet) with ESPHome src={max31865FullImg} alt="" caption="MAX31865 Sensor. Image by Adafruit" layout="constrained" /> As the communication with the MAX31865 is done using SPI, you need to have an spi bus in your configuration with both **miso_pin and mosi_pin** set.

components.name: MAX44009 Ambient Light Sensor
components.description: The max44009 sensor platform allows you to use your MAX44009 (datasheet) ambient light sensor with ESPHome. This sensor features an ultra-wide 22-bit dynamic range from 0.045 lux to 188,000 lux. The I²C bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MAX6675 K-Type Thermocouple Temperature Sensor
components.description: The max6675 temperature sensor allows you to use your MAX6675 thermocouple temperature sensor (datasheet, SainSmart) with ESPHome src={max6675FullImg} alt="" caption="MAX6675 K-Type Thermocouple Temperature Sensor." layout="constrained" /> As the communication with the MAX6675 is done using SPI, you need to have an SPI bus in your configuration with the **miso_pin** set (MOSI is not required).

components.name: MAX6956 I/O Expander
components.description: The MAX6956 component allows you to use MAX6956 I/O expanders (datasheet) in ESPHome. It uses I²C Bus for communication.

components.name: MAX7219 7-Segment Display
components.description: The max7219 display platform allows you to use MAX7219 7-segment display drivers ( datasheet, hobbycomponents) with ESPHome. Please note that this component is only for 7-segment display, not matrix configurations.

components.name: MAX7219 Digit Display
components.description: The max7219 display platform allows you to use MAX7219 digit with ESPHome. Please note that this component is only for the digit "matrix" display, for the 7 segment display see Max7219.

components.name: MAX9611/9612 High Side Current+Voltage+Temperature Sensor
components.description: The MAX9611 sensor platform allows you to use your MAX9611/MAX9612 (datasheet) High-side current, voltage and temperature sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: MCP2515
components.description: The MCP2515 communicates with ESPHome via the SPI bus; to use it, you must have at least one SPI bus with both the mosipin and misopin defined in your ESPHome configuration.

components.name: MCP3008 8-Channel 10-Bit A/D Converter
components.description: The Microchip Technology Inc. MCP3008 devices are successive approximation 10-bit Analog-to-Digital (A/D) converters with on-board sample and hold circuitry.

components.name: MCP3204 & MCP3208 12-Bit A/D Converters
components.description: The Microchip Technology Inc. MCP3204 & MCP3208 devices are successive approximation 12-bit Analog-to-Digital (A/D) converters with on-board sample and hold circuitry. This component is common for both device types. Configure both devices as MCP3204 and observe the maximum number of channels as per variant used.

components.name: MCP3221 12-bit ADC
components.description: The Microchip Technology Inc. mcp3221 sensor platform allows you to use your MCP3221 ADC (datasheet) with ESPHome.

components.name: MCP4461 Component
components.description: The MCP4461 output component enables the use of an 8‑bit external quad digital potentiometer/rheostat via I²C. See the MCP4461 Datasheet for more information.

components.name: MCP4725 Output
components.description: The MCP4725 output component allows to use 12bit external DAC in order to have analog output(s) on any board by using I2C. Devices default address is 0x60 and configurable alternative is 0x61.

components.name: MCP4728 Component
components.description: The MCP4728 output component allows to use 12bit external quad DAC in order to have analog outputs on any board by using I²C. Devices default address is 0x60.

components.name: MCP47A1 Output
components.description: The mcp47a1 output component allows to use 6bit external DAC in order to have analog output(s) on any board by using I²C. Devices default address is 0x2E and configurable alternative is 0x3E.

components.name: MCP9600 Thermocouple Amplifier

components.name: mDNS Component

components.name: MH-Z19 CO_2 and Temperature Sensor

components.name: Micro Wake Word
components.description: ESPHome implements an on-device wake word detection framework from microWakeWord. This repository/library allows you to create a custom wake word for your ESPHome device.

components.name: MicroNova based pellet stove
components.description: The MicroNova component allows you to integrate a pellet stove with a MicroNova board in ESPHome. It uses UART for communication.

components.name: MiCS 4514 Gas Sensor

components.name: Midea Air Conditioner

components.name: Mixer Speaker
components.description: The mixer speaker platform allows you to mix audio sent to different source speakers into one output which is sent to another speaker component. Individual source speakers may be ducked (made quieter) with the apply ducking action.

components.description: The mlx90614 sensor platform allows you to use your Melexis MLX90614 non-contact infrared thermometer (Datasheet, Product Page) with ESPHome. Sensor measures both ambient and object temperature that can be enabled independently.

components.name: MMC5603 Magnetometer

This component allows components in ESPHome to communicate to those devices via RTU protocol. You can access the coils, inputs, holding, read registers from your devices as sensors, switches, selects, numbers or various other ESPHome components and present them to your favorite Home Automation system. This component will track the tank level, distance, temperature, and battery percentage of a Mopeka Standard Check LP BLE device every time the sensor sends out a BLE broadcast.

components.name: MPL3115A2 Barometric Pressure/Altitude/Temperature Sensor

components.description: The mpr121 sensor platform allows you to use your MPR121 (datasheet, Adafruit) Capacitive Touch Sensor with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.description: The mpu6886 sensor platform allows you to use your MPU6886 Accelerometer/Gyroscope (datasheet, M5Stack) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MQTT Client Component
components.description: The MQTT Client Component sets up the MQTT connection to your broker. If you are connecting to Home Assistant, you may prefer to use the native API, in which case this is not needed.

components.name: MQTT Subscribe Sensor
components.description: The mqtt_subscribe sensor platform allows you to get external data into ESPHome. The sensor will subscribe to messages on the given MQTT topic and parse each message into a floating point number.

components.name: MS5611 Atmospheric Pressure Sensor
components.description: The ms5611 sensor platform allows you to use the MS5611 atmospheric pressure-and-temperature sensor with ESPHome (see the datasheet for technical details). The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MS8607 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The ms8607 sensor platform allows you to use your MS8607 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature, pressure and humidity sensors with ESPHome. An I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: MY9231/MY9291 LED driver
components.description: The MY9231/MY9291 component represents a MY9231/MY9291 LED driver chain (MY9231 description, MY9291 description) in ESPHome. Communication is done with two GPIO pins (DI and DCKI) and multiple driver chips can be chained. There are two models with different number of output channels (MY9291 with 4 channels and MY9231 with 3 channels). They are popular driver chips used in smart light bulbs: To use.

components.name: Native API Component
components.description: The ESPHome native API is used to communicate with clients directly, with a highly-optimized network protocol. Currently, only the ESPHome tool, Home Assistant and ioBroker use this native API.

components.name: NAU7802 24-bit ADC
components.description: The nau7802 sensor platform allows you to use your NAU7802 ADC (datasheet, Adafruit) with ESPHome. This ADC is typically used with strain gauges to act as a weight sensor.

components.name: NeoPixelBus Light

As a standard, OpenTherm is independent of any single manufacturer. A controller from manufacturer A can in principle be used to control a boiler from manufacturer B.

components.name: OpenThread Component
components.description: Thread is a low-power mesh networking standard for IoT devices. The low-power aspect is important for battery-powered smart home devices. However, it’s also low-bandwidth, making it ideal for applications that don’t send a lot of data, like switches or motion sensors.

components.name: OPT3001 Ambient Light Sensor

components.description: The pmwcs3 sensor platform allows you to use your PMWCS3 (informations) capacitive soil moisture and temperature sensor with ESPHome. The I²C bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work. Wiring scheme: red is 3.3V, black & shield is GND, green is SDA and white is SCL. 2.2kOhm resistors are advised to pullup both SDA & SDA lines.

components.name: PN532 NFC/RFID
components.description: The pn532 component allows you to use PN532 NFC/RFID controllers (datasheet, Adafruit) with ESPHome. This component is a global hub that establishes the connection to the PN532 via SPI or I²C and outputs its data. Using the PN532 binary sensors you can then create individual binary sensors that track if an NFC/RFID tag is currently detected by the PN532.

components.description: The power_supply component allows you to have a high power mode for certain outputs. For example, if you're using an ATX powersupply to power your LED strips, you usually don't want to have the power supply on all the time while the output is not on. components.name: Prometheus Component
components.description: The prometheus component enables an HTTP endpoint for the Web Server in order to integrate a Prometheus installation.

components.name: Pulse Counter Sensor
components.description: The pulse counter sensor allows you to count the number of pulses and the frequency of a signal on any pin.

components.name: Pulse Meter Sensor
components.description: The pulse meter sensor allows you to count the number and frequency of pulses on any pin. It is intended to be a drop-in replacement for the pulse counter component. Rather than counting pulses over a fixed time interval, the pulse meter sensor measures the time between pulses. The precise manner in which this is done depends on the internalfiltermode option. This leads to a higher resolution, esp.

components.name: Pulse Width Sensor
components.description: The pulse_width sensor allows you to measure how long a given digital signal is HIGH. For example this can be used to measure PWM signals to transmit some value over a simple protocol. The unit of measurement for this sensor is seconds.

components.name: PVVX MiThermometer Display
components.description: The pvvxmithermometer display platform allows you to use devices running the ATCMiThermometer firmware by pvvx as display drivers with ESPHome.

components.name: Pylontech Battery
components.description: The Pylontech component allows you to pull data from Pylontech Batteries into ESPHome. It uses UART for communication.

components.name: QMC5883L Magnetometer
components.description: The qmc5883l allows you to use your QMC5883L triple-axis magnetometers (datasheet) with ESPHome. This sensor is very similar to the HMC5883L sensor and is often found as a knock off replacement. The QMC5883L sensor performs on par to the HMC5883L sensor, though the configuration differs. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: QMP6988 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The qmp6988 sensor platform allows you to use your QMP6988 (datasheet, M5Stack) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Qwiic PIR Motion Binary Sensor
components.description: The Qwiic PIR Motion binary sensor allows you to use your Qwiic PIR (EKMC4607112K based, EKMB1107112 based, firmware documentation) sensors from SparkFun with ESPHome.

components.name: Radon Eye BLE Sensors
components.description: The radoneyerd200 sensor platforms lets you track the output of Radon Eye RD200 Bluetooth Low Energy device.

components.name: RC522 NFC/RFID
components.description: The rc522 component allows you to use RC522 NFC/RFID controllers (datasheet, Ali Express) with ESPHome. ESPHome can read the UID from the tag. Every NFC/RFID tag has a unique "UID" value assigned at the time of manufacture. Tags can be associated with binary sensors, making it easy to determine when a specific tag is present. You can also use the tag information directly within ESPHome automations.

components.name: RDM6300 NFC/RFID
components.description: The rdm6300 component allows you to use RDM6300 NFC/RFID controllers (datasheet, iTead) with ESPHome. This component is a global hub that establishes the connection to the RDM6300 via UART and translates the received data. Using the RDM6300 binary sensors you can then create individual binary sensors that track if an NFC/RFID tag is currently detected by the RDM6300.

components.name: Remote Transmitter
components.description: The remote_transmitter component lets you send various common remote control signals, such as infrared or 433 MHz radio frequency (RF) signals.

components.name: Resampler Speaker
components.description: The resampler speaker platform allows you to convert the sample rate of an audio stream and output it to another speaker component.

components.name: Resistance Sensor
components.description: The resistance platform is a helper sensor that allows you to convert readings from a voltage sensor (such as the ADC Sensor) into resistance readings in Ω (ohm).

components.name: Restart Button
components.description: The restart button platform allows you to restart your node remotely through Home Assistant.

components.name: RF Bridge Component
components.description: The RF Bridge component provides the ability to send and receive 433MHz signals (like RF remotes/key fobs) using radio microcontrollers found on RF bridge devices ( eg., Sonoff RF Bridge).

components.name: RGB Light
components.description: The rgb light platform creates an RGB light from 3 float output components (one for each color channel).

components.name: RGBCT Light
components.description: The rgbct light platform creates an RGBWT (color temperature + white brightness) light from 5 float output components (one for each channel).

components.name: RGBW Light
components.description: The rgbw light platform creates an RGBW light from 4 float output components (one for each channel).

components.name: RGBWW Light
components.description: The rgbww light platform creates an RGBWW (cold white + warm white) light from 5 float output components (one for each channel). The cold and warm white channels can be controlled individually or together, see Mixing for more information.

components.name: Rotary Encoder Sensor
components.description: The rotary_encoder sensor platform allows you to use any continuous-rotation rotary encoders with ESPHome. These devices usually have two pins with which they encode the rotation. Every time the knob of the rotary encoder is turned, the signals of the two pins go HIGH and LOW in turn. See this Arduino article to gain a better understanding of these sensors.

components.name: RP2040 BLE Component
components.description: The rp2040_ble component in ESPHome sets up the Bluetooth LE stack on RP2040 and RP2350 devices using the BTstack library provided by the arduino-pico framework.

components.name: RP2040 PIO LED Strip
components.description: This is a component using the RP2040 PIO peripheral to drive most addressable LED strips.

components.name: RP2040 Platform
components.description: Configuration for the RP2040 platform for ESPHome.

components.name: RPI_DPI_RGB Display Driver
components.description: This display driver supports displays with 16 bit parallel interfaces, often referred to as "RPIDPIRGB" type. These have a parallel interface but no SPI interface and require no configuration of the driver chip.

components.name: RTTTL Buzzer
components.description: The rtttl component allows you to easily connect a passive piezo buzzer to your microcontroller and play monophonic songs. It accepts the Ring Tone Text Transfer Language, RTTTL format (Wikipedia) which allows to store simple melodies.

components.name: Runtime Statistics
components.description: The runtime_stats component allows you to collect and analyze runtime performance statistics for all components in your ESPHome device. This is a powerful debugging and optimization tool that helps identify components that may be blocking the event loop or consuming excessive processing time.

components.name: RuuviTag Open Source BLE Sensor
components.description: The ruuvitag sensor platform lets you track the output of RuuviTag Bluetooth Low Energy devices using the Esp32 Ble Tracker. This component will track the temperature, humidity, acceleration and battery voltage of a RuuviTag device with RAWv1 protocol every time the sensor sends out a BLE broadcast. RAWv2 protocol is supported too. Then tx power, movement count and measurement sequence number are.

components.name: RX8130 Time Source

components.description: Sometimes hardware and/or software doesn't behave as expected. ESPHome supports a "safe mode" to help recover from repeated boot failures/reboot loops. After a specified number (the default is ten) of boot failures, the safe mode may be invoked; in this mode, all components are disabled except serial logging, network (Wi-Fi or Ethernet) and the OTA component(s). In most cases, this will temporaril.

components.name: SCD30 CO₂, Temperature and Relative Humidity Sensor

components.name: SCD4X CO₂, Temperature and Relative Humidity Sensor
components.description: The scd4x sensor platform allows you to use your Sensirion SCD4X CO₂ (datasheet) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: SDL Binary Sensor
