components.name: SDP3x / SDP800 Series Differential Pressure Sensor
components.description: The SDP3x Differential Pressure sensor allows you to use your SDP3x (datasheet, sparkfun) or SDP800 Series (datasheet) sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: SDS011 Particulate Matter Sensor
components.description: The sds011 sensor platform allows you to use SDS011 particulate matter sensors (datasheet) with ESPHome.

components.name: Seeed Studio MR24HPC1 mmWave (Kit)
components.description: The seeed_mr24hpc1 platform allows you to use Seeed Studio 24GHz mmWave Sensor - Human Static Presence Module Lite (Product Page) and Seeed Studio mmWave Human Detection Sensor Kit (Product Page) with ESPHome.

components.description: The servo component allows you to use servo motors with ESPHome. Servos are motor controllers that contain all the electronics necessary for driving the motor and provide a simple PWM interface to control the motor.

components.description: The sfa30 sensor platform allows you to use your Sensirion SFA30 Formaldehyde (datasheet) sensors with ESPHome. However, at the moment only I²C communication is implemented.

components.name: Sharp GP2Y1010AU0F PM2.5 Sensor

A detailed analysis of the Shelly Dimmer 2 hardware is given on arendst/Tasmota#6914.

components.description: The sht3xd sensor platform Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your Sensirion SHT31-D/SHT3x (datasheet, Adafruit) and SHT85 (datasheet, Sensirion) sensors with Esphome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: SHT4X Temperature and Humidity Sensor
components.description: The sht4x sensor platform allows you to use your SHT4X temperature and humidity sensor (datasheet, Adafruit) with ESPHome.

components.name: SHTCx Temperature+Humidity Sensors
components.description: The shtcx sensor platform Temperature+Humidity sensor allows you to use your Sensirion SHTC1 (datasheet, Sensirion SHTC1) and the newer SHTC3 (datasheet, SparkFun) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Shutdown Button
components.description: The shutdown button platform allows you to shutdown your node remotely through Home Assistant. It does this by putting the node into deep sleep mode with no wakeup source selected. After enabling, the only way to startup the ESP again is by pressing the reset button or restarting the power supply.

components.name: Sigma-Delta Output
components.description: Sigma-Delta Output.

components.name: Sim800L Component
components.description: The SIM800L Component provides the ability to dial, answer calls, send/receive SMS text messages and send/receive USSD codes. The device must be connected via a UART bus supporting both receiving and transmitting line. The UART bus must be configured at the same speed of the module which is by default 9600bps. The required connection wires are +VCC, GND, RX and TX.

components.name: Slow PWM Output
components.description: Similar to PWM, the Slow PWM Output platform allows you to control GPIO pins by pulsing them on/off over a longer time period. It could be used to control a heating element through a relay where a fast PWM update cycle would not be appropriate.

components.name: SM16716 LED driver
components.description: The SM16716 component represents a SM16716 LED driver chain (SM16716 description, SM16716 description) in ESPHome. Communication is done with two GPIO pins (MOSI and SCLK) and multiple driver chips can be chained. It is used in some smart light bulbs: To use the channels of this components, you first need to setup the global sm16716 hub and give it an id, and then define the individual output chan.

components.name: SM2135 LED driver
components.description: The SM2135 component represents a SM2135 LED driver chain (SM2135 description, SM2135 description) in ESPHome. Communication is done with two GPIO pins (MOSI and SCLK). It is used in some smart light bulbs: To use the channels of this components, you first need to setup the global sm2135 hub and give it an id, and then define the individual output channels.

components.name: SM2235 LED driver
components.description: The SM2235 component represents a SM2235 LED driver chain in ESPHome. Communication is done with two GPIO pins (DATA and CLK).

components.name: SM2335 LED driver
components.description: The SM2335 component represents a SM2335 LED driver chain in ESPHome. Communication is done with two GPIO pins (DATA and CLK). It is used in some smart light bulbs: To use the channels of this components, you first need to setup the global sm2335 hub and give it an id, and then define the individual output channels.

components.name: SM300D2 7-in-1 Air Quality Sensor
components.description: The sm300d2 sensor platform allows you to use the SM300D2 7-in-1 Air Quality Sensor with ESPHome.

components.name: SML (Smart Message Language)
components.description: The SML component connects to smart meters which use the Smart Message Language (SML) protocol.

components.name: SMT100 Soil Moisture Sensor
components.description: The SMT100 sensor platform allows you to use the SMT100 soil moisture and temperature with ESPHome.

components.name: SN74HC165 I/O Expander (shift register)
components.description: The SN74HC165 component allows you to use SN74HC165 shift registers as I/O expanders (Input only, use SN74HC595 for outputs) (datasheet) in ESPHome. It uses 3 wires (optionally 4) for communication.

components.name: SNTP Time Source
components.description: SNTP Time Source.

components.name: Sonoff D1 Dimmer
components.description: The sonoff_d1 light platform creates a simple brightness-only light for the hardware found in Sonoff D1 dimmer. Installations with Sonoff RM433 433MHz radio remotes are also supported. Use this component to integrate Sonoff D1 dimmer into ESPHome / Home Assistant ecosystem.

components.name: Sound Level Sensor
components.description: The sound_level sensor platform allows you to measure a microphone's average and peak sound pressure levels over a specified measurement duration. The sensors output in relative dB, where 0 dB represents the loudest sound the microphone can measure.

components.name: Speaker Source Media Player
components.description: The speaker_source media player platform orchestrates media source components that produce audio. When a media URL is received, the platform routes it to the appropriate source based on the URI prefix. For example, a URL starting with audio-file:// is handled by the Audio File media source.

components.name: Speed Fan
components.description: The speed fan platform lets you represent any float Output Component as a fan that supports speed settings.

components.name: SPI LED Strip Light
components.description: The spiledstrip light platform drives one or more SPI interfaced RGB LEDs. These LEDs are often used in strips, where each LED is individually addressable. This component requires an SPI interface to be configured.

components.name: SPS30 Particulate Matter Sensor
components.description: The sps30 sensor platform allows you to use your Sensirion SPS30 (datasheet) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work. This sensor supports both UART and I²C communication. However, at the moment only I²C communication is implemented.

components.name: SSD1306 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1306_i2c display platform allows you to use SSD1306 (datasheet, Adafruit), SSD1305 (datasheet), SH1107 (datasheet, Adafruit) and SH1106 (datasheet, electrodragon) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the I²C Bus. If your SSD1306/SSD1305 or SH1106 is connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus, see Over SPI.

components.name: SSD1306 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1306_i2c display platform allows you to use SSD1306 (datasheet, Adafruit), SSD1305 (datasheet), SH1107 (datasheet, Adafruit) and SH1106 (datasheet, electrodragon) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the I²C Bus. If your SSD1306/SSD1305 or SH1106 is connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus, see Over SPI.

components.name: SSD1322 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1322_spi display platform allows you to use SSD1322 (datasheet, Newhaven Display) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus.

components.name: SSD1325/7 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1325_spi display platform allows you to use SSD1325 (datasheet, Adafruit) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus.

components.name: SSD1327 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1327_i2c display platform allows you to use SSD1327 (datasheet, Waveshare) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the I²C Bus. If your SSD1327 is connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus, see Over SPI.

components.name: SSD1327 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1327_i2c display platform allows you to use SSD1327 (datasheet, Waveshare) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the I²C Bus. If your SSD1327 is connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus, see Over SPI.

components.name: SSD1331 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1331_spi display platform allows you to use SSD1331 (datasheet, Adafruit) displays with ESPHome. This component is for displays that are connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus.

components.name: SSD1351 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1351_spi display platform allows you to use SSD1351 (datasheet, Adafruit 128x128, Adafruit 128x96, Waveshare 128x128) displays with ESPHome. This component is for displays that are connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus.

components.name: ST7567 LCD Graphic Display
components.description: The st7567 display platform allows you to use wide range of 128x64 display modules based on Sitronix ST7567 chipset family (ST7567A, ST7567S, ST7567G, etc.) (datasheet, Sitronix) with ESPHome.

components.name: ST7567 LCD Graphic Display
components.description: The st7567 display platform allows you to use wide range of 128x64 display modules based on Sitronix ST7567 chipset family (ST7567A, ST7567S, ST7567G, etc.) (datasheet, Sitronix) with ESPHome.

components.name: ST7701S Display Driver
components.description: This display driver supports displays with 16 bit parallel interfaces, often referred to as "RGB". It currently supports the ST7701S chip.

components.name: ST7735 Display
components.description: A ST7735 display drivers.

components.name: ST7789V TFT LCD
components.description: The st7789v display platform allows you to use ST7789V (datasheet, Tindie) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component utilizes the 4-Wire SPI bus.

components.name: ST7920 LCD Graphic Display
components.description: The st7920 display platform allows you to use ST7920 (datasheet, electrodragon) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the 3-Wire SPI bus. It's a monochrome LCD graphic display.

components.name: StatsD
components.description: StatsD is a protocol to send metrics to a Daemon to store and aggregate them. Today there are many monitoring solutions that support receiving metrics via the StatsD protocol.

components.name: Status Binary Sensor
components.description: The Status Binary Sensor exposes the node state (if it's connected to via MQTT/native API) for Home Assistant.

components.name: Status LED
components.description: The status_led hooks into all ESPHome components and can indicate the status of the device. Specifically, it will: example reading a sensor value fails temporarily, the WiFi/MQTT connections are disrupted, or if the native API component is included but no client is connected.

components.name: STS3X Temperature Sensor
components.description: The sts3x sensor platform Temperature sensor allows you to use your Sensirion STS30-DIS, STS31-DIS or STS35-DIS (datasheet, Sensirion STS3x) sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: STTS22H Temperature Sensor
components.description: The stts22h sensor platform allows you to use a STTS22H temperature sensor (datasheet) with ESPHome. This is a low-power, high-accuracy digital temperature sensor with ±0.5°C accuracy (typical at 25°C) and a temperature range from -40°C to +125°C.

components.name: Sun
components.description: The sun component allows you to track the sun's position in the sky. Calculations are done every 60 seconds.

components.name: SUN_GTIL2 Grid Tie Inverter
components.description: The sun_gtil2 component allows you to get voltage, power and temperature readings from your SUN-1000G2 or SUN-2000G2 grid tie inverter. This is done by reading the UART data transmitted from the inverter's controller board to the display board.

components.name: SX126x Component
components.description: The SX126x component allows you to configure the SX1261, SX1262, SX1268 and LLCC68 transceivers (datasheet) in ESPHome. Transceivers are connected via the SPI Bus. Supported frequencies range from 150 MHz to 960 MHz, including the popular 315 MHz, 433 MHz, 868 MHz, and 915 MHz ISM bands. Supported modulations include LoRa, FSK, GFSK, MSK and GMSK. The SX126x component may be used as a platform for.

components.name: SX127x Component
components.description: The SX127x component allows you to configure the SX1276, SX1277, SX1278 and SX1279 transceivers (datasheet) in ESPHome. Transceivers are connected via the SPI Bus. Supported frequencies range from 137 MHz to 1020 MHz, including the popular 315 MHz, 433 MHz, 868 MHz, and 915 MHz ISM bands. Supported modulations include LoRa, OOK, FSK, GFSK, MSK and GMSK. The SX127x component may be used as a platfo.

components.name: SX1509 16 channel I/O Expander with LED driver and keypad engine
components.description: The SX1509 component allows you to expand your I/O capabilities: (datasheet, SparkFun) in ESPHome. It uses I²C Bus for communication.

components.name: SY6970 Battery Management IC
components.description: The sy6970 component allows you to use the SY6970 battery management and charging ICs SY6970 Datasheet with ESPHome.

components.name: Syslog Component
components.description: The syslog component can be used to send ESPHome logs to a syslog server. It requires both a UDP component and a Time component to be configured.

components.name: T6613/15 CO2 Sensors
components.description: The t6615 sensor platform allows you to use T6613 and T6615 family sensors (amphenol) with ESPHome. T6613 sensors are unreferenced and require ABC to calibrate daily, while the T6615 sensors have a sealed reference gas and do not require ABC calibration. All ppm ranges are supported by this platform.

components.name: TC74 Temperature Sensor
components.description: The TC74 sensor platform allows you to use your TC74 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: TCA9548A I²C Multiplexer
components.description: The TCA9548A component allows you to use TCA9548A as a I²C multiplexer (datasheet, AdaFruit) in ESPHome. It uses I²C Bus for communication.

components.name: TCA9555 I/O Expander
components.description: The TCA9555 component allows you to use TCA955 I/O expanders (datasheet) in ESPHome. It uses I²C Bus for communication.

components.name: TCS34725 RGB Color Sensor
components.description: The tcs34725 sensor platform allows you to use your TCS34725 RGB color sensors (datasheet, Adafruit), color temperature and illuminance sensors with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: TE-M3200 Pressure Sensor
components.description: The tem3200 sensor platform allows you to use your TE-M3200 (datasheet, product page) pressure sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: TEE501 Temperature Sensor
components.description: The hte501 sensor platform Temperature sensor allows you to use your TEE501 (datasheet, E+E TEE501) sensor with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Teleinformation from Linky electrical counter.
components.description: The teleinfo component allows you to retrieve data from a French electrical counter using Teleinformation (datasheet). It works with Linky electrical counter but also legacy EDF electrical counter.

components.name: Template Alarm Control Panel
components.description: The template alarm control panel platform allows you to turn your binary sensors into a state machine managed alarm control panel.

components.name: The Grove - Laser PM2.5 Sensor (HM3301)
components.description: The HM3301 sensor platform allows you to use your HM3301 particulate matter sensor (more info) sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: ThermoPro BLE Sensors
components.description: The thermopro_ble sensor platform lets you track the output of ThermoPro Bluetooth Low Energy devices using the Esp32 Ble Tracker. This component will track the temperature, humidity, battery level and signal strength of the ThermoPro device every time the sensor sends out a BLE broadcast.

components.name: Thermostat Climate Controller
components.description: The thermostat climate platform allows you to control a climate control system in much the same manner as a physical thermostat. Its operation is similar to the Bang-Bang controller; a sensor measures a value (the air temperature) and the controller will try to keep this value within a range defined by the set point(s). To do this, the controller can activate devices like a heating unit and/or a c.

config_entries.label: Fan Only Action Uses Fan Mode Timer

components.name: Time Based Cover
components.description: The time_based cover platform allows you to create covers with position control that do not have any position feedback. The state of the cover is thus always an assumed one, the current position is approximated with the time the cover has been moving in a direction. The state of the cover can be restored at node reboot.

components.name: Time Component
components.description: The time component allows you to set up real time clock time sources for ESPHome. You can then get the current time in lambdas.

components.name: TinyUSB
components.description: The tinyusb component implements a foundation for USB device functionality. It is currently supported on the following ESP32 microcontroller variants: The component simply initializes the TinyUSB driver, allowing the microcontroller to act as a USB device when connected to a USB host.

components.name: TLC59208F
components.description: The TLC59208F component represents a Texas Instruments TLC59208F 8-bit PWM driver (datasheet, hw example) in ESPHome. It uses I²C Bus for communication.

components.name: TLC5947 LED driver
components.description: src="/images/tlc5947.jpg" alt="" caption="Adafruit's TLC5947 board" layout="constrained" width={300} height={225} /> This component represents a chain of TLC5947 24-Channel, 12-Bit PWM LED Drivers, which is used e.g. on this board from Adafruit.

components.name: TLC5971 LED driver
components.description: src="/images/tlc5971.jpg" alt="" caption="Adafruit's TLC59711 board" layout="constrained" width={300} height={225} /> This component represents a chain of TLC5971 12-Channel, 16-Bit PWM LED Drivers, which is used e.g. on this board from Adafruit.

components.name: TM1621 LCD Display
components.description: The tm1621 display platform allows you to use the popular TM1621 LCD display drivers with ESPHome, that can find in Sonoff device like THR316D, THR320D, POWR316D or POWR320D src={tm1621FullImg} alt="" caption="TM1621 LCD Display." layout="constrained" /> The LCD have four signal, cs for chip select, data for data signal, read for reading data dir and write for writing data dir See Rendering Lambda.

components.name: TM1637 7-Segment Display
components.description: The tm1637 display platform allows you to use the popular TM1637 7-segment display drivers with ESPHome.

components.name: TM1638 7 Segment Display Keypad & LED Module
components.description: The tm1638 display platform allows you to use the popular TM1638 7 Segment Display Keypad & LED Module with ESPHome.

components.name: TM1651 Battery Display
components.description: The tm1651 display platform allows you to use battery display units based on the TM1651 chip such as this one with ESPHome. This component supports the level and brightness settings. All updates can be made via lambda expressions.

components.name: TMP102 Temperature Sensor
components.description: The TMP102 Temperature sensor allows you to use your TMP102 (Sparkfun) and LM75 sensors with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: TMP1075 Temperature Sensor
components.description: The TMP1075 Temperature sensor allows you to use your TMP1075 (datasheet) sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: TMP117 Temperature Sensor
components.description: The TMP117 Temperature sensor allows you to use your TMP117 (datasheet, sparkfun) sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: TOF10120 Time Of Flight Distance Sensor
components.description: The tof10120 sensor platform allows you to use TOF10120 optical time of flight sensor (datasheet) with ESPHome to measure distances. The sensor works optically by emitting short infrared pulses and measuring the time it takes the light to be reflected back.

components.name: Tormatic/Novoferm Cover
components.description: Setting up Tormatic and Novoferm covers in ESPHome.

components.name: Total Daily Energy Sensor
components.description: The totaldailyenergy sensor is a helper sensor that can use the power value of other sensors like the HLW8012, HLW8032, CSE7766, ATM90E32, etc and integrate it over time.

components.name: TSL2561 Ambient Light Sensor
components.description: The tsl2561 sensor platform allows you to use your TSL2561 (datasheet, Adafruit) ambient light sensor with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: TSL2591 Ambient Light Sensor
components.description: The tsl2591 sensor platform allows you to use the AMS TSL2591 ambient light sensor with ESPHome. Communication with the device is over I²C, which must be present in your configuration.

components.name: TT21100 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The tt21100 touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the TT21100 chip with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: Tuya MCU
components.description: The tuya component creates a serial connection to the Tuya MCU for platforms to use.

components.name: TX20/TX23 Wind Speed/Direction Sensor
components.description: The tx20 sensor platform allows you to use your TX20/TX23 wind direction and speed sensors with ESPHome. The only required pin is the black or brown cable (which is used to transmit data.) The red cable should be connected to VCC (3.3V), green initiates the data transfer when connected to GND and should therefore be directly connected to GND and the yellow cable should be always connected to groun.

components.name: UART Bus
components.description: UART is a common serial protocol for many devices. For example, when uploading a binary to your ESP you have probably used UART to access the chip. UART (or for Arduino often also called Serial) usually consists of 2 pins: Please note that the naming of these two pins depends on the chosen perspective and can be ambiguous. For example, while the ESP might send (TX ) on pin A and receive (RX ) data.

components.name: UDP Component
components.description: This component allows reception and transmission of data over a network using the User Datagram Protocol (UDP). In conjunction with the Packet Transport Component it can be used to broadcast sensor data.

components.name: uFire ISE pH sensor
components.description: The ufire_ise sensor platform allows you to use your uFire ISE pH sensor with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work. It required also to have an temperature sensor in the liquid tank; this can be on the same board or external sensor linked to the uFire ISE pH configuration.

components.name: uFire Isolated EC sensor
components.description: The ufire_ec sensor platform allows you to use your uFire Isolated EC sensor with ESPHome. The I²C Bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work. It requires also to have a temperature sensor in the liquid tank; this can be on the same board or an external sensor linked to the uFire EC configuration.

components.name: Ultrasonic Distance Sensor
components.description: The ultrasonic distance sensor allows you to use simple ultrasonic sensors like the HC-SR04 (datasheet, SparkFun) with ESPHome to measure distances. These sensors usually can't measure anything more than about two meters and may sometimes make some annoying clicking sounds.

components.name: Uponor Smatrix Base Pulse Underfloor Heating
components.description: The Uponor Smatrix component allows you to integrate an Uponor Smatrix Base Pulse underfloor heating control system in ESPHome without the need for an Smatrix Pulse Com R-208 communication module. It directly communicates with the controller and thermostats via the RS485 thermostat bus.

components.name: USB CDC-ACM Interface
components.description: The USB CDC-ACM (Communications Device Class - Abstract Control Model) component enables supported devices to function as USB virtual serial ports. When connected to a host computer, the microcontroller will appear as one or more serial/COM ports, allowing serial communication with the application running on the microcontroller.

components.name: USB Host Interface
components.description: The USB Host interface on the ESP32-S3 and ESP32-S2 is used to connect to USB peripheral devices. Multiple devices may be configured, but only one can be connected at any time. By default the device must be directly connected to the ESP32, but this can be changed by setting the enable_hubs option to true.

components.name: VBus Component
components.description: The VBus Component provides status reading connectivity to solar heat energy collector controllers using VBus protocol. These devices are mainly produced by Resol, often also found under different brand names like Viessmann, Kioto, Wagner etc. The component currently supports natively the models in the table below but any device can be added via lambda by knowing its packet structure.

components.name: VEML3235 Ambient Light Sensor
components.description: The veml3235 sensor platform allows you to use the Vishay VEML3235 ambient light sensor with ESPHome. Communication with the device is over I²C, which must be present in your configuration.

components.name: VEML7700 and VEML6030 Ambient Light Sensors
components.description: The veml7700 sensor platform allows you to use the Vishay VEML7700 and VEML6030 ambient light sensors with ESPHome. Communication with the device is over I²C, which must be present in your configuration. VEML7700 and VEML6030 are basically the same but in different packages. The VEML7700 uses a fixed address of 0x10, while the smaller VEML6030 can be configured to use either 0x10 or 0x48.

components.name: Version Text Sensor
components.description: The version text sensor platform exposes the ESPHome version the firmware was compiled against as a text sensor.

components.name: VL53L0X Time Of Flight Distance Sensor
components.description: The vl53l0x sensor platform allows you to use VL53L0X optical time of flight (datasheet, ST) with ESPHome to measure distances. The sensor works optically by emitting short infrared pulses and measuring the time it takes the light to be reflected back The sensor can measure distances up to 2 meters, though that figure depends significantly on several conditions like surface reflectance, field of v.

components.name: Voice Assistant
components.description: ESPHome devices with a microphone are able to stream the audio to Home Assistant and be processed there by assist.

components.name: Wake-on-LAN Button
components.description: The wakeonlan button platform allows you to send a Wake-on-LAN magic packet to a computer on the network by specifying its MAC address.

components.name: Waveshare E-Paper Display
components.description: The waveshare_epaper display platform allows you to use some E-Paper displays sold by Waveshare with ESPHome. The 2.13" TTGO module and the Waveshare Cloud Module with an ESP32 on the board are supported as well. Depending on your specific revision of the TTGO board you might need to try out the -b73 or -b1 version (see below). The 1.54" Good Display gdew0154m09 as used in the M5Stack Core Ink is.

components.name: Web Server Component
components.description: The web_server component creates a simple web server on the node that can be accessed through any browser and a simple REST API. Please note that enabling this component will take up a lot of memory and may decrease stability, especially on ESP8266.

components.name: Wiegand keypad and tag reader
components.description: Wiegand-standard key input and card/tag reader panel.

components.name: WiFi Component
components.description: This core ESPHome component sets up WiFi connections to access points for you. You need to have a network configuration (either Wifi or Ethernet) or ESPHome will fail in the config validation stage. You also can't have both Wifi and Ethernet setup in same time (even if your ESP has both wired).

components.name: WiFi Signal Sensor
components.description: The wifi_signal sensor platform allows you to read the signal strength of the currently connected WiFi Access Point.

components.name: WireGuard Component
components.description: Instructions to setup WireGuard for your ESP board.

components.name: WL-134 Pet Tag Sensor
components.description: The wl_134 text sensor platform exposes the last read pet tag as text sensor.

components.name: WTS01 Temperature Sensor
components.description: The wts01 platform allows you to use WTS01 temperature sensors with ESPHome. This is the sensor used in Sonoff TH Origin (THR316, THR320) and TH Elite (THR316D, THR320D) devices.

components.name: X9C Potentiometer Output
components.description: The x9c output platform allows you to add an output that controls a X9C digital potentiometer.

components.name: Xiaomi Mijia BLE Sensors
components.description: The xiaomible sensor platform lets you track the output of Xiaomi Bluetooth Low Energy devices using the Esp32 Ble Tracker. This component will track, for example, the temperature, humidity, moisture, conductivity, illuminance, formaldehyde, mosquito tablet and battery level of the device every time the sensor sends out a BLE broadcast. Contrary to other implementations, xiaomible listens passivel.

components.name: Xiaomi Miscale Sensors
components.description: The xiaomimiscale sensor platform lets you track the output of Xiaomi Bluetooth Low Energy devices using the Esp32 Ble Tracker. This component will track, for example, the weight of the device every time the sensor sends out a BLE broadcast. Contrary to other implementations, xiaomimiscale listens passively to advertisement packets and does not pair with the device. Hence ESPHome has no impact on.

components.name: XL9535 I/O Expander
components.description: The XL9535 component allows you to use XL9535 I/O expander in ESPHome. It uses I²C Bus for communication.

components.name: Z-Wave Proxy
components.description: The zwave_proxy component allows proxying of Z-Wave data frames between a Z-Wave Modem SoC and Z-Wave JS via ESPHome's Api over the Wifi or Ethernet. This allows for more flexibility when placing your Z-Wave hardware within your home.

components.name: Zigbee End Device
components.description: Zigbee End Device for Zigbee2MQTT and ZHA.

components.name: Zio Ultrasonic Distance Sensor
components.description: The Zio Ultrasonic Distance sensor allows you to use your compatible (datasheet, sparkfun) sensors with ESPHome.

components.name: ZyAura CO2 & Temperature & Humidity Sensor
components.description: The ZyAura CO2 & Temperature & Humidity sensor allows you to use your ZGm05(3)(U) MT8057, ZG1683R(U) (MT8060), ZG1583RUD monitors with ESPHome.

components.name: ADE7953 Power Sensor
components.description: > [!NOTE] > This page is incomplete and could use some work. If you want to contribute, please see our > developer site. This page is missing: > > - A complete configuration example for the Shelly 2.5 > - An image for the front page The ade7953 sensor platform allows you to use ADE7953 single phase energy metering ICs (datasheet) with ESPHome. These are commonly found in Shelly devices.

components.name: ADE7953 Power Sensor
components.description: > [!NOTE] > This page is incomplete and could use some work. If you want to contribute, please see our > developer site. This page is missing: > > - A complete configuration example for the Shelly 2.5 > - An image for the front page The ade7953 sensor platform allows you to use ADE7953 single phase energy metering ICs (datasheet) with ESPHome. These are commonly found in Shelly devices.

components.name: Audio File Component
components.description: This component only works on ESP32 based chips.

components.name: Binary Sensor Map
components.description: The binarysensormap sensor platform allows you to map multiple binary sensor to an individual value. Depending on the state of each binary sensor, its associated configured parameters, and this sensor's mapping type, the binarysensormap publishes a single numerical value.

components.name: BME280 Temperature+Pressure+Humidity Sensor
components.description: The bme280 sensor platform allows you to use your BME280 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature, pressure and humidity sensors with ESPHome. The sensor is used in Forced Mode where measurement is performed and then the sensor returns to sleep mode until next measurement. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP280 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp280 sensor platform allows you to use your BMP280 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP280 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp280 sensor platform allows you to use your BMP280 (datasheet, Adafruit) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP388 / BMP390 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp3xx sensor platform allows you to use your BMP388 or BMP390 (datasheet, BMP390 product page) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. Either I²C bus or SPI bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP388 / BMP390 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp3xx sensor platform allows you to use your BMP388 or BMP390 (datasheet, BMP390 product page) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. Either I²C bus or SPI bus is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP581 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp581 sensor platform allows you to use your BMP581 (datasheet, SparkFun or Adafruit) and BMP585 (datasheet) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. Either an I²C bus or an SPI bus are required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: BMP581 Temperature+Pressure Sensor
components.description: The bmp581 sensor platform allows you to use your BMP581 (datasheet, SparkFun or Adafruit) and BMP585 (datasheet) temperature and pressure sensors with ESPHome. Either an I²C bus or an SPI bus are required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Camera Component
components.description: The camera component is the base abstraction layer for camera implementations in ESPHome. It provides a standardized interface between camera hardware/software implementations and the ESPHome API layer.

components.name: Camera Encoder
components.description: The camera_encoder component provides image compression support for software-based cameras or cameras without internal compression. It allows raw camera frames to be compressed into a format suitable for transmission to API clients, such as Home Assistant, which expect JPEG-compressed images.

components.name: Combine the state of several sensors
components.description: The combination sensor platform allows you to combine the state of several sensors into one. To use this sensor, specify the combination type and add your source sensors.

components.name: cst816 Touch Screen Controller
components.description: The cst816 touchscreen platform allows using the touch screen controllers based on the CST816 series of chips with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this touchscreen to work.

components.name: Demo Component
components.description: The demo component can be used for testing to generate sample instances of many different components (sensors, lights, ...) This component has no configuration variables.

components.name: Display Menu
components.description: The component provides a menu primarily intended to be controlled either by a rotary encoder with a button or a five-button joystick controller. It allows to navigate a hierarchy of items and submenus with the ability to change the values and execute commands. The menu can be activated and deactivated on demand, allowing alternating between using the screen for the menu and other information.

components.name: ENS160 Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) and eCO₂ Sensor
components.description: The ens160 sensor platform allows you to use your ENS160 (datasheet) air-quality sensors with ESPHome. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: ENS160 Volatile Organic Compound (VOC) and eCO₂ Sensor
components.description: The ens160 sensor platform allows you to use your ENS160 (datasheet) air-quality sensors with ESPHome. The I²C or SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: ePaper SPI Display
components.description: The epaper_spi display platform provides a new ePaper display component architecture with improved state management and non-blocking operation. This component implements a queue-based state machine that eliminates blocking waits for the busy pin and provides better integration with ESPHome's async architecture.

components.name: ESP32-P4 LDO
components.description: Configuration for the ESP32-P4 LDO peripherals for ESPHome.

components.name: Ethernet Component
components.description: This ESPHome component enables wired Ethernet connections for ESP32 and RP2040 boards.

components.name: Ethernet Info Text Sensor
components.description: The ethernet_info text sensor platform exposes different Ethernet information via text sensors.

components.name: Exposure Notification Listener
components.description: The exposure_notifications component uses the Esp32 Ble Tracker to discover nearby COVID-19 exposure notification bluetooth messages sent by phones running the Google/Apple Exposure Notification service.

components.name: External Components
components.description: You can easily import community or personal components using the external components feature. Bundled components can be overridden using this feature.

components.name: FastLED Light
components.description: > [!WARNING] > FastLED does not work as expected with Arduino 3 or newer for ESP8266. For now, you can either downgrade the arduino version or use Neopixelbus. > > ``yaml > esp8266: > framework: > version: 2.7.4 > `` > > See these related issues: > > - FastLED/FastLED#1322 > - FastLED/FastLED#1264 > [!WARNING] > FastLED does not work with ESP-IDF. > > For addressable lights, you can use Esp32 Rmt.

components.name: Global Variables
components.description: Global Variables.

components.name: Images
components.description: Instructions to display static images on ESPHome.

components.name: Improv via Serial
components.description: The improv_serial component in ESPHome implements the open Improv standard for configuring Wi-Fi on an ESPHome device by using a serial connection to the device, eg. USB.

components.name: INA2xx family of digital power monitors
components.description: src={ina228FullImg} alt="" caption="INA228 Module with integrated shunt from Adafruit" layout="constrained" /> The ina2xx sensor platform allows you to use family of Texas Instruments current and power sensors with ESPHome. Appropriate interface (I²C Bus or SPI Bus) is requred to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: Interval Component
components.description: Interval Component.

components.name: IR Remote Climate
components.description: Controls a variety of compatible Climate devices via IR.

components.name: json Component
components.description: The json component enables ESPHome to work with JSON data in automations, sensors, and HTTP requests. This is particularly useful for: What is JSON?

components.name: LibreTiny Platform
components.description: Configuration for the LibreTiny platform for ESPHome.

components.name: LVGL Binary Sensor
components.description: The lvgl binary sensor platform creates a binary sensor from an LVGL widget and requires LVGL to be configured.

components.name: Mapping Component
components.description: Mapping Component.

components.name: MIPI DSI Display Driver
components.description: Details for the MIPI DSI display driver component in ESPHome.

components.name: MIPI RGB Display Driver
components.description: This display driver supports displays with 16 bit parallel interfaces, often referred to as "RGB". Two classes of display fall under this category, the first are those that only have the RGB interface and require no special configuration of the driver chip. The second are those that have both the RGB interface and an SPI interface which is used to configure the driver chip.

components.name: MIPI SPI Display Driver
components.description: Details for the MIPI SPI display driver component in ESPHome.

components.name: MSA301 and MSA311 Sensors
components.description: The msa3xx sensor platform allows you to use your MSA301 and MSA311 tri-axial, low-g accelerometers (datasheet) with ESPHome. The I²C is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: nRF52 Firmware Updates
components.description: The nRF52 platform supports firmware updates via Over-the-Air (OTA) mechanisms. The only supported bootloader for OTA updates is MCUboot.

components.name: Online Image Component
components.description: With this component you can define images that will be downloaded, decoded and drawn at runtime.

components.name: OpenThread Info Text Sensor
components.description: The openthread_info text sensor platform exposes different OpenThread network information via text sensors.

components.name: Packages
components.description: How to use packages in ESPHome.

components.name: PN532 NFC/RFID
components.description: The pn532 component allows you to use PN532 NFC/RFID controllers (datasheet, Adafruit) with ESPHome. This component is a global hub that establishes the connection to the PN532 via SPI or I²C and outputs its data. Using the PN532 binary sensors you can then create individual binary sensors that track if an NFC/RFID tag is currently detected by the PN532.

components.name: PN7150 NFC
components.description: The pn7150 component allows you to use PN7150 NFC controllers with ESPHome. This component is a global hub that establishes a connection to the PN7150 via I²C.

components.name: PN7160 NFC
components.description: The pn7160 component allows you to use PN7160 NFC controllers with ESPHome. This component is a global hub that establishes a connection to the PN7160 via SPI or I²C.

components.name: PSRAM
components.description: Configuration for the ESP32 PSRAM platform for ESPHome.

components.name: QR Code Component
components.description: Use this component to generate a QR-code containing a string on the device, which can then be drawn on compatible displays.

components.name: Quad SPI Displays
components.description: This display driver supports AMOLED and LCD displays with quad SPI interfaces, using the MIPI DBI interface.

components.name: Remote Receiver
components.description: The remote_receiver component lets you receive and decode various common remote control signals, such as infrared or 433 MHz radio frequency (RF) signals.

components.name: Script Component
components.description: Script Component.

components.name: SN74HC595 I/O Expander (shift register)
components.description: The SN74HC595 component allows you to use SN74HC595 shift registers as I/O expanders (datasheet, SparkFun) in ESPHome. It uses 3 wires (optionally 4) for communication. Optionally, it can be added to a SPI bus to allow sharing pins with other components.

components.name: SPI Bus
components.description: SPI is a very common high-speed protocol for a lot of devices. The ESPHome SPI component implements only the host controller role, where it controls the bus, and writes or reads data from peripherals attached to the bus.

components.name: Sprinkler Controller
components.description: The sprinkler controller component aims to behave like a sprinkler/irrigation valve controller, much like those made by companies such as Rain Bird or Hunter. It does so by automating control of a number of switch components, each of which would typically be used to control an individual electric valve via a relay or other switching device. It provides a number of features you'd expect of a sprink.

components.name: SSD1306 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1306_i2c display platform allows you to use SSD1306 (datasheet, Adafruit), SSD1305 (datasheet), SH1107 (datasheet, Adafruit) and SH1106 (datasheet, electrodragon) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the I²C Bus. If your SSD1306/SSD1305 or SH1106 is connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus, see Over SPI.

components.name: SSD1322 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1322_spi display platform allows you to use SSD1322 (datasheet, Newhaven Display) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus.

components.name: SSD1325/7 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1325_spi display platform allows you to use SSD1325 (datasheet, Adafruit) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus.

components.name: SSD1327 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1327_i2c display platform allows you to use SSD1327 (datasheet, Waveshare) displays with ESPHome. Note that this component is for displays that are connected via the I²C Bus. If your SSD1327 is connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus, see Over SPI.

components.name: SSD1331 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1331_spi display platform allows you to use SSD1331 (datasheet, Adafruit) displays with ESPHome. This component is for displays that are connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus.

components.name: SSD1351 OLED Display
components.description: The ssd1351_spi display platform allows you to use SSD1351 (datasheet, Adafruit 128x128, Adafruit 128x96, Waveshare 128x128) displays with ESPHome. This component is for displays that are connected via the 4-Wire SPI bus.

components.name: ST7567 LCD Graphic Display
components.description: The st7567 display platform allows you to use wide range of 128x64 display modules based on Sitronix ST7567 chipset family (ST7567A, ST7567S, ST7567G, etc.) (datasheet, Sitronix) with ESPHome.

components.name: Substitutions
components.description: How to use substitutions in ESPHome.

components.name: Uptime Sensor
components.description: The uptime sensor allows you to track the time the ESP has stayed up for in seconds. Time rollovers are automatically handled.

components.name: USB Host UART Interface
components.description: This component allows an ESP32-S3 or ESP32-S2 to host USB-serial peripheral devices. It uses the Usb Host component to interface to the device as a USB-OTG host.

components.name: Web Server Component
components.description: The web_server component creates a simple web server on the node that can be accessed through any browser and a simple REST API. Please note that enabling this component will take up a lot of memory and may decrease stability, especially on ESP8266.

components.name: WeiKai SPI/I²C UART/IO Expander
components.description: WeiKai Microelectronics provides a family of UART & GPIO expansion chips that interfaces to a micro-controller through SPI or I²C bus.

components.name: WeiKai SPI/I²C UART/IO Expander
components.description: WeiKai Microelectronics provides a family of UART & GPIO expansion chips that interfaces to a micro-controller through SPI or I²C bus.

components.name: WeiKai SPI/I²C UART/IO Expander
components.description: WeiKai Microelectronics provides a family of UART & GPIO expansion chips that interfaces to a micro-controller through SPI or I²C bus.

components.name: WiFi Info Text Sensor
components.description: The wifi_info text sensor platform exposes different WiFi information via text sensors.

components.name: Xiaomi Miscale2 combined into Xiaomi Miscale
components.description: Xiaomi Miscale2 combined into Xiaomi Miscale.

components.name: XPT2046 Touch Screen Controller (Updated version)
components.description: The xpt2046 touchscreen platform allows using the resistive touch screen controllers based on the XPT2046 chip (datasheet, AZ-Delivery) with ESPHome. Many cheap LCD displays contain this controller. The SPI is required to be set up in your configuration for this sensor to work.

components.name: XXTEA Component
components.description: Lightweight encryption algorithm for ESPHome.
